paper.title_en: Topological phase transition driven by Hatsugai-Kohmoto interaction on the Kagome lattice
paper.abstract_en: The interplay between band topology and strong correlations is central to modern condensed matter physics, but exact solutions are rare. Here, we present an exactly solvable model on the Kagome lattice by combining a Kane-Mele-type spin-orbit coupling with the Hatsugai-Kohmoto interaction. At 1/3 filling, we uncover a continuous topological quantum phase transition driven by electron interaction. A weakly correlated $\mathbb{Z}_2$ topological insulator gives way to a strongly correlated insulator that, while $\mathbb{Z}_2$-trivial, hosts a nontrivial spin Chern number $C_s=2$. The transition exhibits critical scaling consistent with the universality class of two-dimensional Dirac fermions. At half-filling, the same model yields a non-Fermi-liquid to Mott-insulator transition, demonstrating that correlation-driven topological and Mott transitions can be unified within a single solvable framework. Our results establish the Kagome Hatsugai-Kohmoto model as a valuable benchmark for interacting topological systems.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文提出了一个精确可解的Kagome点阵模型，结合了Kane-Mele型自旋轨道耦合和Hatsugai-Kohmoto相互作用，展示了由电子相互作用驱动的拓扑量子相变，提供了对强关联拓扑系统的宝贵基准，理论深度高且方法严谨。
decision.title_zh: Kagome点阵上Hatsugai-Kohmoto相互作用驱动的拓扑相变
decision.abstract_zh: 能带拓扑与强关联之间的相互作用是现代凝聚态物理的核心，但精确解很少。在这里，我们通过将Kane-Mele型自旋轨道耦合与Hatsugai-Kohmoto相互作用结合，在Kagome点阵上提出了一个精确可解的模型。在1/3填充下，我们发现了一个由电子相互作用驱动的连续拓扑量子相变。一个弱关联的Z2拓扑绝缘体让位于一个强关联绝缘体，该绝缘体虽然Z2平凡，但拥有非平凡的旋Chern数Cs=2。该相变表现出与二维狄拉克费米子普适性类一致的临界标度行为。在半填充下，同一模型产生了一个从非费米液体到莫特绝缘体的相变，表明关联驱动的拓扑相变和莫特相变可以在一个单一的可解框架内统一。我们的结果确立了Kagome Hatsugai-Kohmoto模型作为相互作用拓扑系统的一个宝贵基准。
